name: Wan2GP (riogesulgon fork) — simple (no image build)
dockerStartCmd: if [ ! -d /workspace/Wan2GP/.git ]; then git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/riogesulgon/Wan2GP /workspace/Wan2GP; fi; exec bash /workspace/Wan2GP/runpod/bootstrap.sh
readme:
# Wan2GP (riogesulgon fork) — simple, no image build

Clone-and-run template for the riogesulgon/Wan2GP fork (deepbeepmeep/Wan2GP v12.34 + durable generation queue). Uses the public `pytorch/pytorch:2.7.0-cuda12.8-cudnn9-devel` base image — **no custom image build, no registry, no DinD**.

On pod start the start command clones the fork onto the persistent /workspace volume and runs `runpod/bootstrap.sh`, which installs torch 2.10+cu128 + requirements, then starts the Gradio UI on port 7862.

## Durable queue

The pending queue is written to `/workspace/queue.zip` on every mutation and on SIGTERM, and auto-restores in a new browser session — so client disconnects, crashes, and RunPod pod stops no longer lose your queued jobs. Stop the pod, start it again, open a fresh browser tab → the queue autoloads.

## First boot

- pip install of torch + requirements takes ~10–15 min (one-time; pip cache lives on the volume so subsequent boots are fast).
- First generation downloads the Wan 2.1 I2V 14B model to `/workspace/Wan2GP/ckpts` (one-time; persists on the volume).
- SageAttention is NOT installed by default (Wan2GP falls back to flash/SDPA attention — works, slightly slower). To enable it later, run inside the pod:
  `cd /workspace/Wan2GP && pip install --no-build-isolation git+https://github.com/thu-ml/SageAttention.git`
  (the devel base image has nvcc, so it can compile for the pod's GPU).

## Use

- Open **Connect → HTTP [7862]** for the Gradio UI.
- Use **Connect → Web Terminal** for shell access (no sshd needed).
- Target: **Wan 2.1 I2V 14B**.

See the repo's `runpod/bootstrap.sh`, `QUEUE_PERSISTENCE_PLAN.md`, and `runpod/RUNBOOK.md`.
